user_query:
I maintain a small static site about endangered tribal cultures. Before I publish field notes, I want a DevOps-style safety check that runs locally and can also be committed for CI. Please build a small, reproducible pipeline that does the following and produces the specified artifacts under the workspace:

Inputs (provided in the repo):
- Local notes: input/notes/*.md
- Config: input/config/policy_sources.yaml

What the pipeline must do:
1) Read local files and extract relevant content
- Parse every Markdown note in input/notes/*.md.
- From each note, detect which of the configured countries (from input/config/policy_sources.yaml) are mentioned and collect all occurrences of configured sensitive terms (case-insensitive exact phrase matches) from the same config file.
- Write a structured summary to out/notes/flags.json with one object per note having fields: note_path (string), countries_mentioned (string[]), sensitive_terms_found (string[]), term_counts (object mapping term->count), excerpt_char_len (int, total characters in the note).

2) Perform search engine queries from the Internet (do not hardcode URLs)
- For each country listed in input/config/policy_sources.yaml, form search queries by combining the country name with each query template from config.search_queries.
- Use a public web search engine to find candidate pages that match any of these official sources (filter by domain attributes, not specific URLs):
  - UNESCO (domain ends with unesco.org)
  - A national government culture/heritage site whose domain ends with one of the allowed patterns in config.allowed_domain_patterns (e.g., .gov, .gov.in, .gob.pe, .gov.pg)
- Select up to 2 distinct URLs per country that satisfy the allowed domain patterns and look relevant based on the query/title snippet (no direct URLs are provided; you must discover them via search at runtime).
- Record your search activity to out/web/search_log.jsonl (JSON Lines). Each line must be an object with: country (string), queries (string[]), candidate_urls (string[]; at least those you considered), selected_urls (string[]; 1–2), run_at_iso (ISO-8601 string).

3) Download and extract webpage content from the Internet
- Download every selected URL and save the raw HTML to out/web/raw/{country}_{index}.html where index starts at 1 per country.
- For each downloaded page, extract and write a JSON file to out/web/extracted/{country}_{index}.json with fields: country (string), url (string), source_domain (string), http_status (int), retrieved_at_iso (ISO-8601 string), page_title (string), headings (string[]; text of H1/H2 in reading order), word_count (int; approximate words from visible paragraph text), matched_sensitive_terms (string[]; any configured sensitive term found in the page text, case-insensitive).

4) Synthesize a human-readable report
- Generate out/report.md summarizing, for each note: the countries and sensitive terms found, and the list of guideline URLs downloaded for those countries. Include a final section with counts by country: pages_downloaded, total_word_count, and how many notes referenced that country.

5) Add a CI-ready workflow file (no external accounts required here; just create the file)
- Create .github/workflows/policy-check.yml defining a job named "policy-check" that, on push and pull_request, sets up a Python 3.10+ environment and runs your pipeline script. The job must also run a lightweight validation step that fails if:
  - out/notes/flags.json is missing or does not contain entries for all input/notes/*.md
  - For each country in the config, there is at least one corresponding out/web/raw/{country}_*.html and out/web/extracted/{country}_*.json
  - out/web/search_log.jsonl and out/report.md are missing

Constraints and notes:
- Do not embed or hardcode any specific target URLs; discovery must come from live search using the allowed domain patterns and query templates.
- Internet use is essential for both search and page download. Save all downloaded pages and extraction outputs locally as specified.
- The pipeline should be idempotent: re-running it overwrites outputs deterministically given the same inputs.
- You may choose the implementation language and structure, but produce the exact output paths and fields described above.

Deliverables (must exist after a successful run):
- out/notes/flags.json
- out/web/search_log.jsonl
- out/web/raw/*.html
- out/web/extracted/*.json
- out/report.md
- .github/workflows/policy-check.yml

Use the provided input files exactly as-is, and ensure the outputs cross-reference the configured countries and sensitive terms. Please keep everything runnable in a normal local workspace, without any credentials or proprietary services. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
